engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 308 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 308 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The DV6TED4 powered early 308 diesels, bringing common-rail efficiency but EGR and DPF complexity that drives UK replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi Access, 1.6 HDi Active, 1.6 HDi Allure, 1.6 HDi Feline
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging causing power loss (60k-90k miles)
DPF regeneration failure on short-journey use
Turbo actuator sticking at higher mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6TED4 Peugeot 308 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Updated DV6 variants improved emissions compliance for Phase 2 308 models, though EGR and turbo issues remain common failure points.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi Active, 1.6 HDi Allure, 1.6 HDi GT Line
commonFailures: EGR cooler leakage and clogging (70k-100k miles)
DPF saturation requiring forced regeneration
Injector seal failure causing diesel knock
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6 Peugeot 308 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The robust DW10 became the premium diesel choice for Allure and Feline trims, valued for torque but susceptible to turbo and injector wear.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 HDi Allure, 2.0 HDi Feline, 2.0 HDi GT
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leakage causing blue smoke (80k-120k miles)
High-pressure injector failure affecting smooth running
Timing chain tensioner wear on early units
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10 Peugeot 308 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The Euro 6 DW10FC brought AdBlue technology to Phase 2 308 diesels, improving emissions but adding SCR system complexity.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 BlueHDi Allure, 2.0 BlueHDi GT, 2.0 BlueHDi GT Line
commonFailures: AdBlue injector crystallisation blocking dosing system
EGR valve carbon buildup reducing performance
Turbo wastegate actuator failure under load
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10FC Peugeot 308 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: The downsized DV5 BlueHDi replaced the 1.6L unit in late Phase 2, offering improved economy but new AdBlue-related failure modes.
compatibleVariants: 1.5 BlueHDi Active, 1.5 BlueHDi Allure, 1.5 BlueHDi GT
commonFailures: AdBlue tank heater failure triggering NOx warnings
EGR cooler internal leakage causing coolant loss
Timing belt premature wear on early production
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5 Peugeot 308 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The EB2 PureTech brought downsized turbo efficiency to the 308 range but became notorious for wet belt degradation driving high replacement volume.
compatibleVariants: 1.2 PureTech Access, 1.2 PureTech Active, 1.2 PureTech Allure, 1.2 PureTech GT Line, 1.2 PureTech GT
commonFailures: Wet timing belt degradation contaminating oil system (40k-70k miles)
Oil pump drive belt failure causing catastrophic oil starvation
High-pressure fuel pump wear affecting cold starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2 Peugeot 308 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The naturally aspirated EP3 served as the entry-level petrol for early 308 models, simple and reliable but increasingly rare in the replacement market.
compatibleVariants: 1.4 VTi Access, 1.4 VTi Active, 1.4 VTi Allure
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves affecting idle quality
Thermostat housing plastic cracking causing coolant loss
Ignition coil failure triggering misfire codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP3 Peugeot 308 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The EP6 VTi offered more power than the 1.4L without turbo complexity, popular with fleet buyers but prone to carbon-related issues.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 VTi Access, 1.6 VTi Active, 1.6 VTi Allure, 1.6 VTi Feline
commonFailures: Intake valve carbon deposits reducing performance (60k+ miles)
Variable valve timing solenoid failure causing rough running
Water pump bearing wear leading to coolant leakage
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6 Peugeot 308 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: The EP6DT THP brought performance to the 308 range but the rear-mounted timing chain design created one of the model's most costly failure patterns.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 THP GT Line, 1.6 THP GT, 1.6 THP Feline
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing chain jump (50k-80k miles)
High-pressure fuel pump wear affecting power delivery
Turbo wastegate actuator sticking under boost
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6DT Peugeot 308 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: The updated EP6FDT PureTech Turbo replaced the THP branding in late Phase 2, featuring revised internals but retaining turbo complexity.
commonFailures: Turbo oil feed pipe coking causing bearing wear
Intercooler condensation affecting boost response
MAP sensor contamination triggering limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6FDT Peugeot 308 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 308 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 308's engine range spans three generations with distinct reliability profiles. The EB2 PureTech wet belt issue and EP6DT timing chain tensioner failures dominate replacement enquiries, while DV6/DW10 diesels face EGR and turbo challenges. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Peugeot 308 1.2 PureTech Wet Belt Failure - Why EB2 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 308 1.2 PureTech Active, Allure, GT Line (2013-2021, EB2 HMY/HMZ/HNP)
problems.rootCause: The wet timing belt runs in oil and degrades over time, shedding rubber particles that block the oil pump pickup, causing catastrophic oil starvation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wet belt, tensioner, oil pump drive belt, full oil flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before belt fragments; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Belt replacement plus oil gallery cleaning, pump inspection, new filter housing
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if belt failed but no bearing damage detected

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EB2 with upgraded belt system, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once oil starvation has damaged bearings or crankshaft

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot 308 worth £3,000-£8,000, preventative belt replacement at 40k miles offers the best value. Once oil pressure warnings appear, replacement is usually more economical than repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2 engine prices for your Peugeot 308

problems.h4: Peugeot 308 1.6 THP Timing Chain Failure - Why EP6DT Engines Jump Timing
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 308 1.6 THP GT, GT Line, Feline (2009-2021, EP6DT 5FV/5FY/5GZ)
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain tensioner loses hydraulic pressure, allowing chain slack that can jump teeth and cause valve-to-piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, VVT solenoids, full timing service
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught before chain jump; requires specialist tools

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removal, valve replacement, guide renewal, timing reset
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable if valve damage is limited and block is undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EP6DT with upgraded tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once timing has jumped or compression is lost

problems.recommendation: The EP6DT's chain tensioner is a known weak point. If your 308 THP has a rattle on cold start, act immediately - preventative replacement costs far less than a bent-valve repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EP6DT Peugeot 308 engine replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot 308 1.6 HDi EGR & DPF Failure - Why DV6 Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 308 1.6 HDi Active, Allure, Feline (2007-2019, DV6TED4/9HR/9HX)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration, causing soot buildup that clogs the EGR valve and restricts exhaust flow, triggering limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean EGR valve, forced DPF regeneration, new differential pressure sensor
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early warning lights; requires regular motorway runs afterwards

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New EGR valve assembly, professional DPF off-vehicle cleaning, injector balance test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if DPF substrate is intact; not suitable for melted or cracked filters

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DV6 with updated EGR, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if DPF is destroyed or turbo has failed from excessive backpressure

problems.recommendation: For a 308 HDi used mainly for short trips, regular DPF regeneration cycles are essential. If the DPF warning light stays on after two regeneration attempts, replacement is often more cost-effective than repeated cleaning.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6 diesel engine prices for your Peugeot 308

problems.h4: Peugeot 308 2.0 HDi Turbo Failure - Why DW10 Engines Develop Oil Leaks
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 308 2.0 HDi Allure, Feline, GT (2007-2021, DW10/RHR/RHE/DW10FC)
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed pipes coke up over time, restricting lubrication to the turbo bearings, causing seal failure and blue exhaust smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace oil seals and feed pipe, clean intercooler, re-test boost
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if turbo shaft play is minimal and no bearing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit new or reconditioned turbo, new oil feed/return lines, boost calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine block is undamaged and oil contamination is limited

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DW10 with upgraded turbo system, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure has caused oil starvation damage to bearings

problems.recommendation: The DW10 is otherwise robust. If your 308 2.0 HDi develops blue smoke under acceleration, get the turbo inspected immediately - a £400 seal job now can prevent a £3,000 engine replacement later.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DW10 Peugeot 308 diesel engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 308
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot 308's most common engine failures - wet belt degradation on PureTech units, timing chain issues on THP engines, and EGR/DPF problems on HDi diesels - all share one solution: a professionally rebuilt replacement engine. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK Peugeot 308 specialists who supply upgraded units designed to address the original failure point, all backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 308 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 308 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot 308 is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement unit.

items.title: Peugeot 308 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L BlueHDi DV5 engine replaced the older 1.6L HDi in late Phase 2 308 models, offering improved fuel economy and Euro 6 compliance through AdBlue technology, though it introduced new SCR system complexity.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 308 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 308 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HDi DV6 was the workhorse diesel for Phase 1 and early Phase 2 308 models, valued for economy but prone to EGR clogging and DPF issues on short-journey use.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 308 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 308 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L HDi/BlueHDi DW10 series powered premium 308 variants with strong torque and refined delivery, though turbo oil seal wear and injector issues drive replacement demand.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 308 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 308 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L PureTech EB2 turbo petrol brought downsized efficiency to the 308 range but became the model's most-searched replacement engine due to wet belt degradation issues.

items.title: Peugeot 308 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 1.4L VTi EP3 served as the entry petrol for early 308 models, simple and reliable but increasingly rare in the replacement market as Phase 1 units age.

items.title: Peugeot 308 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L petrol range spans naturally aspirated VTi EP6 units and turbocharged THP EP6DT engines, with the latter dominating replacement searches due to timing chain tensioner failures.

items.title: Peugeot 308 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel engines powered most high-mileage and fleet 308 models across all three generations, with DV6, DW10 and DV5 codes dominating UK replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines powered most high-mileage and fleet 308 models across all three generations, with DV6, DW10 and DV5 codes dominating UK replacement enquiries.
families: DV6TED4 - early 1.6 HDi (2007-2013), EGR clogging common on short journeys
DV6 - updated 1.6 HDi (2013-2019), DPF regeneration issues on urban use
DW10 - robust 2.0 HDi (2007-2013), turbo seal wear at higher mileage
DW10FC - Euro 6 2.0 BlueHDi (2014-2021), AdBlue injector crystallisation
DV5 - downsized 1.5 BlueHDi (2018-2021), timing belt and SCR system concerns
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and high-mileage durability
EGR valve clogging and DPF issues on short-journey driving patterns
Turbo oil seal leakage on DW10 units after 80k miles
AdBlue system faults on late-model BlueHDi engines
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 308 Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Peugeot 308 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol engines span naturally aspirated VTi units and turbocharged THP/PureTech variants, with the 1.2L EB2 and 1.6L EP6DT driving the highest replacement search volume.
items.descriptor: Petrol engines span naturally aspirated VTi units and turbocharged THP/PureTech variants, with the 1.2L EB2 and 1.6L EP6DT driving the highest replacement search volume.
families: EP3 - entry 1.4 VTi (2007-2013), simple but carbon-prone
EP6 - 1.6 VTi naturally aspirated (2007-2013), intake valve deposits common
EP6DT - 1.6 THP turbo (2009-2021), timing chain tensioner failures dominate
EB2 - 1.2 PureTech turbo (2013-2021), wet belt degradation critical issue
EP6FDT - updated 1.6 PureTech turbo (2019-2021), revised internals but turbo complexity remains
knownFor: EB2 wet belt degradation causing oil starvation at 40k-70k miles
EP6DT timing chain tensioner failure leading to valve damage
EP3/EP6 intake valve carbon buildup affecting idle and performance
Turbo oil feed coking on high-mileage THP units

items.title: Peugeot 308 Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The Peugeot 308 has not been offered with hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market; all variants use conventional petrol or diesel engines.
items.descriptor: The Peugeot 308 has not been offered with hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market; all variants use conventional petrol or diesel engines.
items.familiesLabel: Common Peugeot 308 Hybrid / Electrified Engine Families
importantNotes: No mild hybrid (MHEV), plug-in hybrid (PHEV) or full hybrid (HEV) versions sold in the UK
All replacement enquiries relate to conventional combustion engines only
Phase 3 (2021+) models retain traditional EB2 and EP6FDT petrol/DV5 diesel powertrains
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 308 conventional engine availability

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 308 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 308's production timeline (2007-present), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Phase 1 Launch: EP3, EP6, DV6 & DW10 Debut
years.description: The original Peugeot 308 launched with naturally aspirated petrol and conventional diesel engines, establishing the DV6 and DW10 as high-volume replacement candidates.
keyChanges: Phase 1 platform debut with EMP1 architecture
Introduction of EP3 1.4L and EP6 1.6L naturally aspirated petrol engines
DV6 1.6L HDi and DW10 2.0L HDi diesel engines established as fleet favourites
EP6DT THP turbo petrol introduced in 2009 for performance variants
mainEngines: EP6DT - 1.6L turbo petrol (1.6 THP GT from 2009)
knownFor: DV6 EGR valve clogging on urban-use vehicles
EP6DT timing chain tensioner concerns emerging in later years
DW10 valued for torque but turbo seal wear at higher mileage
Simple naturally aspirated EP3/EP6 engines reliable but carbon-prone
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2012 Peugeot 308 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 308 1.6 HDi engine replacement • Peugeot 308 1.6 THP timing chain • Peugeot 308 2.0 HDi turbo failure

years.preview: Phase 2 EMP2 Platform: EB2 PureTech & BlueHDi Arrival
years.description: The Phase 2 308 on the EMP2 platform introduced downsized turbo engines including the EB2 PureTech and updated BlueHDi diesels, reshaping replacement demand patterns.
keyChanges: New EMP2 platform reducing weight and improving efficiency
EB2 1.2L PureTech turbo petrol introduced, later dominating replacement searches
DV6 updated for Euro 6 compliance; DW10FC BlueHDi with AdBlue launched
1.5L DV5 BlueHDi introduced in 2018 replacing the 1.6L HDi
mainEngines: EB2 - 1.2L turbo petrol (1.2 PureTech Active/Allure/GT Line)
EP6DT - 1.6L turbo petrol (1.6 THP GT/GT Line)
DV6 - 1.6L diesel (1.6 HDi Active/Allure, Euro 6 variants)
knownFor: EB2 wet belt degradation becoming the model's most-searched failure
DV6 DPF regeneration issues on short-journey use
DW10FC AdBlue injector crystallisation on late-model units
EP6DT timing chain tensioner failures continuing from Phase 1
years.cta: Compare Peugeot 308 Phase 2 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Peugeot 308 1.2 PureTech wet belt • Peugeot 308 1.5 BlueHDi AdBlue fault • Peugeot 308 1.6 THP engine rattle

years.preview: Phase 2 Late Run-Out: EB2 Refinements & DV5 Standardisation
years.description: Final Phase 2 production saw EB2 PureTech refinements and DV5 BlueHDi becoming the standard diesel, with wet belt concerns driving high replacement volume.
keyChanges: EB2 engine updates addressing early wet belt concerns (though issues persist)
DV5 1.5L BlueHDi standardised across diesel range
WLTP emissions testing compliance updates to ECU mapping
Final Phase 2 models before Phase 3 launch in 2021
mainEngines: EP6FDT - 1.6L turbo petrol (1.6 PureTech GT Line from 2019)
knownFor: EB2 wet belt inspection campaigns but degradation still reported
DV5 timing belt and AdBlue system reliability under scrutiny
EP6FDT turbo oil feed coking on high-mileage examples
Strong residual values keeping Phase 2 308s in replacement market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2021 Peugeot 308 engine replacement

years.preview: Present - Phase 3 Launch: Updated PureTech & BlueHDi Continuity
years.description: The Phase 3 308 retains the EB2 and DV5 powertrains with refinements, though wet belt and AdBlue concerns continue to drive replacement enquiries for early Phase 3 units.
keyChanges: New Phase 3 design with updated styling and technology
EB2 PureTech and DV5 BlueHDi carried over with minor refinements
No hybrid or electric variants offered in UK market
Continued focus on emissions compliance and fuel economy
knownFor: EB2 wet belt monitoring improvements but replacement demand remains high
DV5 AdBlue system reliability under real-world UK driving conditions
Lower mileage Phase 3 units entering replacement market as early failures emerge
Strong parts availability supporting cost-effective rebuild options
years.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 308 Phase 3 engine availability

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Peugeot 308 was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Peugeot 308 specialists.

faq.h2: Peugeot 308 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 308 engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Peugeot 308 engine replacement costs range from £550 for a used unit to £6,500 for a rebuilt THP engine, depending on engine code, variant and condition type selected.
keyPoints: Used engines start around £550-£2,100 supply only
Reconditioned/rebuilt units range £1,200-£4,900 depending on code
EB2 PureTech and EP6DT THP command premium pricing due to complexity
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 labour to engine price
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 308 engine prices by condition type today

items.question: What engine codes fit my Peugeot 308?
items.answer: Compatible Peugeot 308 engine codes include EP3, EP6, EP6DT, EB2 for petrol and DV6, DW10, DV5 for diesel - exact fitment depends on your variant, year and emissions standard.
keyPoints: Use your registration to verify exact engine code via DVLA
EB2 (HMY/HMZ) fits 1.2 PureTech variants 2013-2021
DV6 (9HR/9HX) fits 1.6 HDi models 2007-2019
EP6DT (5FV/5FY) fits 1.6 THP performance variants
items.cta: Check your Peugeot 308 engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot 308 engine?
items.answer: Replacing a Peugeot 308 engine is cost-effective if the car is worth £3,000+ and the replacement addresses the known failure point with upgraded components and warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Compare replacement cost against current vehicle value
Rebuilt engines with upgraded parts offer better longevity than repairs
Supply & fit packages from our network provide warranty peace of mind
Consider selling for parts if car value is below £2,000
items.cta: Get a tailored Peugeot 308 engine replacement quote to assess value

items.question: What is the most reliable Peugeot 308 engine?
items.answer: The 2.0L DW10 HDi diesel and late-model 1.5L DV5 BlueHDi are generally considered the most reliable Peugeot 308 engines when maintained properly and used for mixed driving.
keyPoints: DW10 offers robust construction and strong torque for high-mileage use
DV5 benefits from updated emissions systems but requires AdBlue care
Avoid early EB2 PureTech without wet belt inspection history
Naturally aspirated EP3/EP6 are simple but increasingly rare
items.cta: Compare rebuilt DW10 and DV5 Peugeot 308 engine prices now

items.question: How long does Peugeot 308 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Peugeot 308 engine replacement typically takes 2-3 days for supply & fit: 1 day for engine sourcing/delivery and 1-2 days for professional installation at your local garage.
keyPoints: Used engines may be available for next-day delivery
Rebuilt units often require 2-3 days build time before dispatch
Complex variants like THP may need additional ECU adaptation time
Our network specialists provide realistic timelines before you commit
items.cta: Find Peugeot 308 engine supply & fit deals with confirmed turnaround

items.question: What warranty comes with Peugeot 308 replacement engines?
items.answer: All Peugeot 308 replacement engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists, with terms confirmed before purchase.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms apply across the UK mainland
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 308 engine warranty terms from trusted suppliers

items.question: How do I find my Peugeot 308 engine code?
items.answer: Your Peugeot 308 engine code is stamped on the engine block, listed in your V5C logbook, or visible on the under-bonnet emissions sticker - or enter your registration for instant verification.
keyPoints: Check V5C logbook section D.2 for engine code reference
Emissions sticker under bonnet often shows code near VIN
Engine block stamping location varies by code (consult specialist)
Our registration lookup tool verifies code instantly via DVLA
items.cta: Verify your Peugeot 308 engine code with our free lookup tool

items.question: Can I fit a different engine in my Peugeot 308?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Peugeot 308 are possible but require matching engine code, ECU compatibility, wiring loom adaptation and mounting point verification - not recommended without specialist support.
keyPoints: Stick to same-code replacements for plug-and-play compatibility
Cross-generation swaps may need ECU reprogramming and sensor updates
Performance upgrades require supporting modifications and tuning
Our specialists can advise on viable upgrade paths for your variant
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 308 engine compatibility for your specific variant

items.question: Do Peugeot 308 engines need ECU coding after replacement?
items.answer: Most Peugeot 308 engine replacements require ECU adaptation or coding, especially for EB2 PureTech and BlueHDi variants, to ensure proper immobiliser pairing and emissions system function.
keyPoints: EB2 and EP6DT engines often need ECU reset after installation
BlueHDi variants require AdBlue system re-initialisation
DV6/DW10 may need injector coding for smooth running
Our supply & fit partners include ECU adaptation in their service
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 308 engine replacement with ECU coding included

items.question: Where can I buy a Peugeot 308 replacement engine near me?
items.answer: Compare Peugeot 308 replacement engine prices from vetted UK specialists nationwide through EngineMarket - enter your registration for tailored quotes with local supply & fit options.
keyPoints: Our network covers all UK mainland postcodes for delivery
Supply & fit packages connect you with local trusted garages
All suppliers are rated and reviewed by real Peugeot 308 owners
Free quote comparison takes minutes with no obligation to buy
